alloy_matches.reason: Same alloy name, base element, and composition; aliases match.

sample_matches.reason: Both describe equiaxed austenite grains, annealing twins, same grain size, dislocation density, and yield strength.

truth.papers.A75.title: Effects of Cryogenic Rolling on the Microstructure Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Low-Density Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C Steel
truth.papers.A75.paper_scope: Systematic investigation of the influence of cryogenic rolling deformation on microstructure evolution and mechanical properties of low-density Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C steel.

prediction.papers.A75.title: Effects of Cryogenic Rolling on the Microstructure Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Low-Density Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C Steel
prediction.papers.A75.paper_scope: Systematic investigation of the influence of cryogenic rolling deformation on microstructure evolution and mechanical properties of low-density Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C steel, covering dislocation structures, twinning, texture evolution, and strength-ductility trade-off.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe28mn8al1c.alloys_notes: Measured chemical composition (wt%): C 0.97, Mn 27.91, Al 8.04, S 0.004, P 0.006, Fe Bal. Density determined as 6.82 g cm⁻³.

truth.processes.proc_st.description: Solution treatment at 1000°C for 1 h followed by water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_cr.description: Solution treatment at 1000°C for 1 h and water quenching, then cryogenic rolling with cumulative thickness reductions of 30%, 50%, 70%, and 90%.

prediction.processes.proc_st.description: Vacuum melting and casting, hot forging at 1200°C for 2 h into 100×100 mm billets, solution treatment at 1000°C for 1 h and water quenching; undeformed state.
prediction.processes.proc_st.processes_notes: Plate-shaped specimens excised from billet center prior to solution treatment.
prediction.processes.proc_cr.description: Base process followed by cryogenic rolling (CR) to 90% cumulative thickness reduction via liquid nitrogen immersion and repeated single-pass rolling (~2% per pass).

truth.processing_steps.sample_undeformed.proce: Prior to this step, the ingot was heated at 1200°C for 2 h and hot forged into billets.
truth.processing_steps.sample_cr30.processing_: Specimens immersed in liquid nitrogen for 3–5 min before each pass; single pass reduction ~2%; repeated cycles until cumulative 30% reduction.
truth.processing_steps.sample_cr50.processing_: Specimens immersed in liquid nitrogen for 3–5 min before each pass; single pass reduction ~2%; repeated cycles until cumulative 50% reduction.
truth.processing_steps.sample_cr70.processing_: Specimens immersed in liquid nitrogen for 3–5 min before each pass; single pass reduction ~2%; repeated cycles until cumulative 70% reduction.
truth.processing_steps.sample_cr90.processing_: Specimens immersed in liquid nitrogen for 3–5 min before each pass; single pass reduction ~2%; repeated cycles until cumulative 90% reduction.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_undeformed.proce: Water-quenched to room temperature after solution treatment.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_cr30.processing_: Single-pass rolling with ~2% thickness reduction per pass, re-immersed in liquid nitrogen between passes until cumulative reduction reached 30%.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_cr50.processing_: Single-pass rolling with ~2% thickness reduction per pass, re-immersed in liquid nitrogen between passes until cumulative reduction reached 50%.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_cr70.processing_: Single-pass rolling with ~2% thickness reduction per pass, re-immersed in liquid nitrogen between passes until cumulative reduction reached 70%.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_cr90.processing_: Single-pass rolling with ~2% thickness reduction per pass, re-immersed in liquid nitrogen between passes until cumulative reduction reached 90%.

truth.structures.sample_undeformed.overall_str: Equiaxed austenite grains with straight grain boundaries, containing annealing twins and a few dislocation lines.
truth.structures.sample_cr30.overall_structure: Austenite grains elongated along RD with many intersecting slip bands; Taylor lattice structure formed by slip band intersections.
truth.structures.sample_cr30.microstructure_co: Slip bands present; dislocation density increased; Taylor lattice.
truth.structures.sample_cr30.microstructure_li: slip bands and increased dislocation density
truth.structures.sample_cr50.overall_structure: Elongated/flattened grains with dense slip bands, dislocation walls, and extensive deformation twins.
truth.structures.sample_cr50.microstructure_co: Dislocation walls and deformation twins prominent.
truth.structures.sample_cr70.overall_structure: Elongated grains with larger dislocation cells and reduced twin density.
truth.structures.sample_cr90.overall_structure: Fibrous morphology; nanocrystalline austenite grains with random orientations, micro-shear bands, intersecting slip bands, and few twins.
truth.structures.sample_cr90.microstructure_co: Average grain size 58.8 nm; continuous SAED rings confirm nanocrystalline structure.
truth.structures.sample_cr90.microstructure_li: brass {110}<112> component dominates (copper component at low strain changes to brass)

prediction.structures.sample_undeformed.overall_str: Single-phase austenite with equiaxed grain morphology
prediction.structures.sample_undeformed.microstruct: random orientation with 94.65% HAGBs and 60°<111> twin boundaries
prediction.structures.sample_cr30.overall_structure: Elongated austenite grains with slip bands and Taylor lattice
prediction.structures.sample_cr50.overall_structure: Flattened or banded austenite grains with dislocation walls and deformation twins
prediction.structures.sample_cr50.microstructure_li: dislocation entanglement, dislocation walls, and deformation twins
prediction.structures.sample_cr70.overall_structure: Elongated austenite grains with larger dislocation cells and reduced twin density
prediction.structures.sample_cr70.microstructure_li: elongated grains with increased slip band density
larger dislocation cells with high dislocation density, diminished twin density
prediction.structures.sample_cr90.overall_structure: Nanocrystalline austenite with fibrous morphology and micro-shear bands
prediction.structures.sample_cr90.microstructure_li: fibrous nanocrystalline grains with random orientation
high-density dislocations, wave-like micro-shear bands, and intersecting slip bands

truth.characterization_methods.sample_undeform: OM, TEM, EBSD, XRD, tensile test, microhardness
Microstructural and mechanical characterization of undeformed solution-treated steel
Equiaxed austenite grains (~40.3 μm), annealing twins, random texture, UTS 882 MPa, YS 505 MPa, elongation 64.5%, hardness 284 HV.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr30.tec: OM, TEM, EBSD, XRD, tensile test, microhardness
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr30.key: Elongated grains, slip bands, Taylor lattice, copper texture, increased dislocation density, UTS 1261 MPa, YS 1092 MPa, elongation 13.6%, hardness 435 HV.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr50.tec: OM, TEM, XRD, tensile test, microhardness
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr50.key: Dense slip bands, dislocation walls, deformation twins, UTS 1518 MPa, YS 1379 MPa, elongation 8.8%, hardness 479 HV.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr70.tec: OM, TEM, XRD, tensile test, microhardness
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr70.key: Dislocation cells, reduced twin density, UTS 1693 MPa, YS 1588 MPa, elongation 7.2%, hardness 530 HV.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr90.tec: OM, TEM, EBSD, XRD, tensile test, microhardness, SEM fracture
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr90.key: Fibrous morphology, nanocrystalline grains (~58.8 nm), brass texture, micro-shear bands, UTS 1861 MPa, YS 1791 MPa, elongation 6.6%, hardness 578 HV, fracture shows mixed ductile-brittle.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_undeform: Dislocation lines and grain boundaries in undeformed state.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr30.key: Dimple size decreases compared to undeformed state; fracture surface becomes flatter.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr90.key: Partial cleavage fracture features; transition to ductile-brittle mixed fracture mode.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Trend of calculated dislocation density agrees with TEM observations.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Separate grain size and microstrain contributions to XRD peak broadening; obtain microstrain and grain size for each deformation condition

ε, D, b (Burgers vector not provided in text)

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Grain size results validated against TEM dark-field images
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: XRD peak broadening analysis to separate grain refinement and microstrain contributions
Equation (1): β_τ = Kλ/(D cos θ) + 4ε tan θ; applied to FWHM data of austenitic peaks.
Calculate dislocation density from average grain size and microstrain
Equation (2): ρ = 2√3 (ε²)^½ / (D b); results range from 3.2×10¹⁴ to 23.6×10¹⁴ m⁻².
σ_s = σ₀ + k_s d⁻½ for slip; σ_T = σ₀′ + k_T d⁻½ for twinning, where k_T > k_s for FCC metals.
Explain dislocation strengthening during tensile deformation
Classical Taylor hardening describing increased flow stress due to dislocation forest formation.

Θ, m, σ, ε, ε̇_P, T
Equation (3): Θ ≤ σ(1−m), where Θ is strain hardening rate and m is strain rate sensitivity factor.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The alloy density was measured as 6.82 g cm^{-3}.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Stacking fault energy (SFE) of this steel at room temperature is 72 mJ m^{-2} and at cryogenic temperature is 51 mJ m^{-2}.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: The critical stress for dislocation slip and twinning follow Hall–Petch relationships, with k_T > k_s, explaining reduced twinning at refined grain sizes.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Elongation values differ between text (64%) and Figure 8b table (64.5%); table values used.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: DF-TEM grain size analysis yields an average of 58.8 nm, but Figure 3f caption states 50.8 nm — discrepancy likely due to different measurement areas.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Fracture mode transitions from ductile (large dimples) to mixed ductile-brittle (cleavage features) with increasing CR reduction.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Texture evolves from copper {112}<111> at low strain to brass {110}<112> at high strain (CR 90%).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Equation (3) (Hart's necking criterion) is discussed to explain ductility loss.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Tensile dog-bone specimens had a gauge segment of 20 × 5 × 1 mm and were machined along the rolling direction; tensile tests were conducted at a crosshead speed of 0.2 mm min⁻¹.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Microhardness measurements were performed with a 500 g load and 15 s dwelling time, with reported HV values averaged from at least 5 measurements.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Figure 8a provides engineering stress–strain curves for all deformation levels, but continuous curve data are not available in the provided text pipeline without image pixels.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The stacking fault energy (SFE) of the steel is reported as 72 mJ m⁻² at room temperature and 51 mJ m⁻² at cryogenic temperature.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fracture morphology transitions from ductile fracture with large and deep dimples in the undeformed state to a ductile-brittle mixed fracture mode with partial cleavage features at 90% CR reduction.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Texture evolution from copper {112}<111> at CR 30% to brass {110}<112> at CR 90% was observed via EBSD.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: At CR 90%, wave-like micro-shear bands with high internal dislocation density and nanocrystalline grains with random orientation (continuous SAED rings) were observed.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: XRD analysis confirmed that no deformation-induced martensitic phase transformation occurred during the entire cryogenic rolling process.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The undeformed state exhibited 94.65% HAGBs; after 30% CR, LAGBs increased to 89.4%; after 90% CR, LAGBs accounted for 94.1%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: The density of the test steel was determined to be 6.82 g cm⁻³.
